hacker_news.title: Tell HN: Meta's AI support feature allows Instagram accounts to be stolen
hacker_news.author: parable
hacker_news.content_context:
If the AI support option is enabled for your Instagram account (it appears to be A/B tested for only a percentage of accounts), anyone can hijack it with little effort. Simply get on a proxy or VPN close to the account's region, then ask the agent to send a code to an arbitrary email address. Once you receive the code, pass it forward to the agent, and it'll provide you with a password reset link which you can then use to sign into the account.

Posting here for any Meta employees who may be reading. This flaw has been around for at least a few days and has been used to hijack over 100 high-value Instagram accounts. The correct patch would be to disable the AI support feature entirely for the time being until this is sorted and revert accounts and usernames that have been hijacked over the last few days. This is a pretty important flaw and it's currently being exploited in blackhat circles. The steps above are public knowledge in these circles and can be found trivially on Telegram.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: TIL – a timeline file format with a spiral viewer and editor
hacker_news.author: rellfy
hacker_news.summary: Spiral timeline viewer and editor for the .til format.

linked_context.page_description: Spiral timeline viewer and editor for the .til format.

hacker_news.content_context:
Hi HN. til is a timeline file format and CLI with a web viewer and editor.

A timeline stores 3 main things: events, ranged events, and tags.

Events can point to external refs (URIs, UUIDs, etc; a ref is an arbitrary string) and also contain arbitrary JSON attributes. The idea is that the .til file works as a temporal index, where payloads, notes etc live wherever you already keep them.

I've wanted to build this for a while, mostly for keeping track of and viewing timelines that I find interesting (music, roman empire stuff, etc). This project has 3 main elements, which are all independent: the file format itself (binary, built with postcard), a rust CLI, and a web viewer + editor (react, with the parser compiled to WASM so the FE shares the same implementation).

Source: https://github.com/rellfy/til Spiral timeline viewer and editor for the .til format.

hacker_news.title: Simple systems are the best systems
hacker_news.author: jerodsanto
hacker_news.summary: When it comes to systems design, John Gall knew his stuff. He even has a LAW1 about it: A complex system that works is invariably found to have evolved from a simple system that worked. A complex system designed from scratch never works and cannot be patched up to make it work. You have to start over with a working simple system. Gall isn’t the only promoter of simple systems. Here’s Dijkstra (maybe you’ve heard of him?)

top_trends.title: Tell HN: Meta's AI support feature allows Instagram accounts to be stolen
top_trends.author: parable
top_trends.content_context:
If the AI support option is enabled for your Instagram account (it appears to be A/B tested for only a percentage of accounts), anyone can hijack it with little effort. Simply get on a proxy or VPN close to the account's region, then ask the agent to send a code to an arbitrary email address. Once you receive the code, pass it forward to the agent, and it'll provide you with a password reset link which you can then use to sign into the account.

Posting here for any Meta employees who may be reading. This flaw has been around for at least a few days and has been used to hijack over 100 high-value Instagram accounts. The correct patch would be to disable the AI support feature entirely for the time being until this is sorted and revert accounts and usernames that have been hijacked over the last few days. This is a pretty important flaw and it's currently being exploited in blackhat circles. The steps above are public knowledge in these circles and can be found trivially on Telegram.

named_hotspots.name: Markitdown
named_hotspots.what_happened: 微软在GitHub上开源的Python工具Markitdown登上趋势榜，单日获2759星，总星数达134791，关注度显著上升。
named_hotspots.core_details: Markitdown是一个由微软开发的Python工具，专门用于将各类文件及Office文档（如Word、Excel、PPT）转换为Markdown格式。项目由afourney、gagb等多位贡献者维护，目前以Python语言实现，在GitHub上已获得大量关注与使用。

source_briefs.item_name: Show HN: Monolith, live typed queries on real Postgres for Java (no JDBC)
source_briefs.content_summary: Monolith是一套小型Java库，用于在PostgreSQL上构建应用。它通过单一声明生成模式、类型化数据访问、客户端读取器和实时（响应式）查询。其核心目标是让Java团队在不放弃关系型数据库的前提下，获得类似Firebase的实时数据更新体验。目前为v0.1实验版本。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 将响应式编程与关系数据库结合的新尝试，可能简化实时应用的开发复杂度。

source_briefs.item_name: Tell HN: Meta's AI support feature allows Instagram accounts to be stolen
source_briefs.content_summary: 用户报告Meta的AI客服功能存在严重安全漏洞：若账户启用了此功能（目前为A/B测试），攻击者可通过代理或VPN接近账户所在区域，要求AI客服向任意邮箱发送验证码，从而获取密码重置链接并劫持账户。据称该漏洞已存在数日，导致超过100个高价值Instagram账户被黑。建议Meta暂时禁用该功能并恢复受影响账户。
source_briefs.ai_comment: AI客服的身份验证逻辑存在缺陷，凸显了AI系统在安全流程中集成时需严格测试。

source_briefs.content_summary: MAPA.UA是一个乌克兰实时空袭警报交互式地图服务，显示无人机、导弹、制导炸弹等对平民的威胁。数据持续更新，支持PWA推送通知，覆盖iOS、Android和桌面浏览器。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 利用实时数据可视化提升公共安全，是技术在人道主义应用中的典型案例。

source_briefs.content_summary: 经过一周的抗议活动，新泽西州移民拘留中心的家庭探视已部分恢复。当地已实施宵禁，并有抗议者与ICE人员冲突的照片流出。

source_briefs.item_name: Cory Booker Has ‘Concerns’ About Platner Revelations in Maine Senate Race - The New York Times
source_briefs.content_summary: 缅因州参议院候选人Graham Platner被揭露在已婚期间向女性发送性暗示短信，其妻子曾向竞选团队披露此事。参议员科里·布克对此表示担忧。
